title: fix(text): strip cache_control from content parts
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Speaking the same language
gist.blurb: We've smoothed out a quirk where Anthropic-style messages were confusing strict OpenAI-compatible models. Your prompts now flow perfectly, no matter which client you're using.
gist.summary: Strips the cache_control field from message content parts before forwarding requests to upstream providers.
gist.impact: Users can now seamlessly use Anthropic-shaped clients like Claude Code with strict OpenAI-compatible models without encountering 400 errors.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a 'cache_control' data block to clean up a stream of code, representing the removal of the incompatible field. The bee mascot supervises the data cleanup.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a glowing 'cache_control' block from a stream of code, while the cute bee mascot watches with a tiny clipboard. A retro terminal nearby glows lime green (#ecf874). Soft pastel gradient background. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
